title: ADRD Driver Diagram
text:
Evidence-Based Brain Health System Framework — Key Driver Diagram





  Evidence-based brain health system framework
  Key Driver Diagram




  
    Aim statement
    Adults—beginning in midlife—experience equitable, timely, and coordinated cycles of risk identification, early detection, follow-up, and action because evidence-informed brain health approaches are embedded within existing systems, supported by clearly defined and reliable community–clinical linkages, and actively stewarded by public health to maintain alignment and continuity across partners.
  

  
    5Primary drivers
    
    16Secondary drivers
    
    24Core activities
    
    70Supporting activities
  

  
    Key:
    PD 1 — Governance
    PD 2 — Data & learning
    PD 3 — Navigation
    PD 4 — Financing
    PD 5 — Community capacity
    Supporting activities

title: Julia Chevan
text:
Julia Chevan, PT, DPT, PhD, MPH

Hi, I am Julia and I am a physical therapist, health services researcher, and public health practitioner with more than 30 years of experience at the intersection of academia, rehabilitation, implementation science, and chronic disease prevention. 
Currently I serve as Senior Program Manager with the National Association of Chronic Disease Directors. My day-to-day work focuses on building the evidence for clinical-community partnerships that improve outcomes for people living with arthritis, fall risk, and other chronic conditions. In July 2025, I will be joining the Clinical Research PhD Program at the University of Jamestown as faculty.
To learn more about building your Quarto website visit https://quarto.org/docs/websites.
